Fires anytime during market hours. Catches gap-downs / news shocks before EOD.

iOS PUSH NOTIFICATIONS (PWA)
Receive alerts even when Option Panda is closed via Apple's Web Push gateway. Requires a free OneSignal account (10,000 subscribers free) and a one-time setup. Real-time pushes work when the iPhone is locked or the app is backgrounded.
iOS requirement: add Option Panda to your home screen first (Safari → Share → Add to Home Screen), then open from the home-screen icon. iOS 16.4+ only allows push for installed PWAs.
▾ STEP 1 — Get a OneSignal App ID (3 min)
  1. Go to onesignal.com → sign up (free)
  2. Create new app → choose Web Push as the platform
  3. Site URL: https://alexreed122287.github.io/scanner/
  4. Site Name: Option Panda
  5. Default Icon URL: https://alexreed122287.github.io/scanner/assets/icon-192.png
  6. Choose Custom Code integration (NOT WordPress)
  7. Copy the App ID (looks like 11111111-2222-3333-4444-555555555555) and paste below ↓
▾ STEP 4 — Wire pushes to scanner alerts (advanced)
The scanner now calls opPushSend(title, body) in addition to the local browser notification when a HIGH-CONVICTION alert fires. To deliver pushes from a backend (so they fire when the app is closed):
  1. In OneSignal dashboard → Settings → Keys & IDs → copy your REST API Key
  2. POST to https://onesignal.com/api/v1/notifications from any backend (curl, Cloudflare worker, scheduled-tasks MCP) with body:
    {"app_id":"YOUR_APP_ID","included_segments":["All"],"headings":{"en":"NVDA hit GO"},"contents":{"en":"Score 152 · $920.50 · click for details"}}
    with header Authorization: Basic YOUR_REST_KEY
  3. Or use the included opPushSend() client-side helper from the browser console while the app is open — sends via OneSignal's anonymous Player ID for the current device.
HIGH-CONVICTION ALERT SETTINGS
Fires when a ticker matches: PROVEN + Score ≥90 + Analyst Revisions ↑ + GEX Call Heavy
Alert triggers on scan completion and auto-scan (if enabled). Browser must be open.
Show desktop/mobile popup when signal fires
Send email via EmailJS (free, 200/mo)
EmailJS is free (200 emails/mo). Setup: emailjs.com → create account → add Email Service → create Template → copy IDs above.
Template variables available: {{ticker}}, {{score}}, {{sector}}, {{price}}, {{signal_time}}, {{rules_passed}}
/ 122 — GO signals above this score trigger alerts
minutes — prevents repeat alerts for same ticker
AUTO-EXECUTION SETTINGS
When signal fires, fetch best contract and pre-fill Order Ticket — one click to submit
contracts to pre-fill (overridden by budget field if set)
Auto-submit after countdown — order fires unless you cancel
seconds before auto-submit fires (5–60)
⚠ Auto-Submit executes a real order at your broker when enabled in LIVE mode. Only enable if you are actively monitoring the terminal. The countdown modal has a CANCEL button. Sandbox mode is always safe.
AUTO-TRADER — FULLY AUTOMATED EXECUTION OFF
Auto-Trader Master Switch
Scans every 60s • Opens positions on High Conviction signals • Arms trail stop on fill
per trade — passed to step fill as budget limit
hard cap — prevents over-buying cheap options (budget still applies)
auto-disarms if cumulative loss on auto-trades exceeds this
/ 240 — score is the only gate
8:45 AM – 2:45 PM CST only (recommended)
stop opening new positions when this many are already open
if step fill doesn't fill after 11 attempts, submit a market order to guarantee entry

⚠ LIVE MODE WARNING: When enabled in Live mode, the Auto-Trader will automatically submit real orders at your broker without further confirmation. Ensure your position size limit and daily loss limit are set correctly. The browser tab must remain open. Orders use step fill: 11 attempts, 110% bid-ask coverage. If Market Order Fallback is enabled, a market order fires automatically when the step fill doesn't fill. Trail stop is armed automatically on fill.

▲ OPTION PANDA — HOW TO
A walkthrough of every tab, what each setting does, and the order to use them in. Read top-to-bottom on first visit.
▶ QUICKSTART — 5 MINUTES TO YOUR FIRST SCAN
  1. Open the API tab. Paste your Tradier API key (live or sandbox). Optionally paste an FMP key for fundamentals (analyst grades). Pick mode (sandbox/live). Click SAVE KEYS.
  2. Switch to FULL SCANNER. Default filters are already calibrated: Min Score 160, Signal=GO, Min Price $1, Min AvgVol 500,000.
  3. Click ↻ SCAN. Bulk pass scores ~1500 tickers in <30s. The 60 highest get deep enrichment (Tradier history + FMP). Background pass fills ranks 61–200 over the next ~2 minutes.
  4. Look for green GO badges on rows scoring ≥160. The ★ HIGH CONVICTION preset (top of strategy row) further filters to rows that ALSO have PROVEN + Analyst Up + GEX Call Heavy.
  5. Click any row to open its detail panel (right side on desktop, bottom sheet on iPhone). Click ▲ BUY to open the contract popup; pick a strike + expiry, then ▲ BUY NOW launches the Order Ticket pre-filled.
  6. Manage open positions from the POSITIONS tab. Enable Trailing Stop and Smart Exits at the bottom of that tab to auto-close on adverse moves.
Pro tip: Page reloads preserve your scan results, GEX data, positions, and trail-stop config via localStorage — no re-fetch on refresh. Cache TTLs: scan results 30min, GEX 24h, positions 5min.
▶ FULL SCANNER TAB

Purpose: The primary tab. Scores ~1500 tickers across 14+ technical and fundamental rules, ranks them, and surfaces the ones most likely to move.

Sticky header bar (always visible)

  • ▶ STRATEGY · ▶ FILTERS · ▶ MUST PASS · ▶ SCREENER — four collapsible filter sections. Click any segment to expand. State persists per-session.
  • ↻ SCAN — runs the scan. Click anytime regardless of which sections are open. Disabled mid-scan.
  • ▲ EXPORT GOs — copies a comma-separated list of all GO-tier tickers to clipboard.
  • Last scan timestamp — when the visible data was generated.
  • ⚠ data gaps chip — appears if the bulk pass didn't fully enrich the top-200. Hover for details (Tradier history N/200, FMP fundamentals N/200, GEX coverage). Background pass usually closes these gaps within 2 minutes.

STRATEGY presets

  • ★ HIGH CONVICTION — PROVEN + Score ≥160 + Analyst Revisions ↑ + GEX Call Heavy. ~67% of max score; rarest setups.
  • ▲ BREAKOUT — Near 52-week high + RS > SPY + MACD + + TF Aligned 2/3.
  • ▶ MOMENTUM — MACD + + Above EMA200 + Vol > Avg + RS > SPY.
  • ◆ BEST ITM CALLS — Score ≥180 + liquid + contract ≤$8. Deep-ITM (δ 0.70–0.90) candidates.
  • ▲ PRE/POST MARKET — same filters as HIGH CONVICTION but the table re-sorts by absolute % gap so the biggest movers float to the top. Use during 3:00–8:30am CST (pre) or 3:00–7:00pm CST (post).
  • VERIFY — diagnostic only. Pings Tradier with 5 sentinel tickers and shows the raw `change_percentage`/`last_volume`/`trade_date` fields so you can confirm extended-hours data is flowing through your proxy.
  • ✕ CLEAR — removes any active preset. Restores score-based ranking.

FILTERS row

  • Sectors — multi-select. Empty = all. Useful for narrowing scans to specific industries.
  • Themes — multi-select industry themes (e.g. "AI Infrastructure", "Memory & Storage"). Hydrated from theme_scores.json. Click any chip in the scrolling ribbon at the top to filter to just that theme.
  • Signal — GO / NO-GO / All.
  • Min Score — composite score floor. Default 130 (calibrated to observed distribution mid-top). Range 0–240. When a strategy preset is selected, this gets overwritten with that preset's own threshold (HC=140, BO=125, MO=120, ITM=145).
  • FILTER / CLEAR — applies / resets the criteria.

MUST PASS chips

Each checkbox forces the corresponding rule to pass for a ticker to appear. Stack as needed. The chip set is grouped by tier:

  • Tier 1 (highest IC, +12 to +20 score points): JT 12-1 Mom, Minervini, Pocket Pivot, VCP, Donchian 20d. These were exposed as MUST-PASS chips on 2026-05-03 — previously they only contributed to the score. Use them to enforce the published-research signals directly instead of relying on a score threshold to be a proxy for them.
  • Tier 2 (confirmation, +5 to +10): 52Wk Hi <15%, RS > SPY, MACD +, TF Aligned, > EMA200, Vol > Avg.
  • Tier 3 (overlay / fundamental): Analyst Revisions ↑, GEX Call Heavy, PROVEN, Strong Sector.
  • Hard filters: No Earnings <Nd (binary-event exclusion).

Recommended Tier-1 stacks for hand-rolled (no preset) screens

  • Pure Trend (academic): JT 12-1 + > EMA200. Adds Asness/Frazzini momentum on top of Minervini's Stage 2 floor.
  • Coiled Spring: VCP + Pocket Pivot + Vol > Avg. Tight contraction → institutional accumulation print.
  • Channel Breakout: Donchian 20d + Vol > Avg + RS > SPY. Classic Turtle setup with leadership confirmation.
  • Wizard Buy: Minervini + 52Wk Hi <15% + RS > SPY. Full SEPA template — Mark Minervini's "Trade Like a Stock Market Wizard" buy criteria.
  • Belt-and-suspenders: JT 12-1 + Minervini + Pocket Pivot. All three highest-IC scoring rules required — typically 0-3 names, but every hit has the strongest published edge.

SCREENER row

  • Min Price / Max Price — $1+ default. Set Max Price if you only buy small-caps.
  • Min AvgVol — formatted with commas. 500,000 default. Lower = more candidates, but illiquid contracts.
  • Today Vol % — only show tickers whose today's volume is at least N% of average. Catches volume spikes.
  • Max Opt $ — filter out tickers whose ATM option is too expensive.
  • SAVE / RESET — persist your filter snapshot to localStorage / restore factory defaults.

Results table

  • # rank — sort priority is: score → techScore → RS 5D → theme dailyScore → volume. The "↓ X" annotation shows which factor decided that row's position vs the row above. (Pre/Post preset overrides this with absolute gap %.)
  • SYMBOL — click to open detail panel. Click again to close. The RUN pill next to a symbol means GEX hasn't been computed for that ticker (it's outside the top-200 batch). Click RUN to fetch single-ticker GEX (~3s).
  • SCORE — composite out of ~240 max points.
  • SIGNAL — GO (green, score ≥ Min Score) or NO-GO.
  • BUY — opens the contract popup with all calls ranked by liquidity × OI score.
  • THEME / SECTOR / PRICE / CHG % / VOLUME / TECH / FUND / RS 5D — sortable columns. Click any header to sort.
▶ POSITIONS TAB

Purpose: Live view of your open Tradier positions plus auto-exit configuration.

Header row

Total P&L · Day P&L · Open Positions · Opened Today · Cash · Account Value plus REFRESH and CLEAR buttons all on one line. Click RESET next to Total P&L to zero the lifetime counter (snapshots current Tradier realized P&L as the new baseline).

Position table

Sorted by DTE ascending (soonest expiry first); STOCK rows pinned to bottom. Columns: TICKER · OPENED · CLOSE · ROLL · QTY · PRICE (current contract mid) · EQUITY (qty × price × multiplier) · P/L $ · P/L % · STRIKE · EXP · DTE · STOCK PRICE · ENTRY PRICE · B/E · 1/2 ATR · 1 ATR · EMERGENCY EXIT · TRAIL.

The three ATR columns (1/2, 1, EMERGENCY) are computed at entry time from the underlying's ATR(14) on the day you first saw the position — they NEVER recompute as the stock moves. They refresh only on a roll.

▲ TRAILING STOP (below table)

Per-position trailing stop with a global poller. Configure activate %, pullback %, and close mode (step fill / market order). Master toggle at top; per-row overrides via the EDIT ▾ button in the TRAIL column. Auto-arm on load is opt-in.

⚠ SMART EXITS (below trailing stop)

Three independent rules that auto-close based on the underlying's behavior:

  • RULE 1 — INTRADAY EMERGENCY: close any time during market hours if the underlying moves N×ATR adverse from entry.
  • RULE 2 — EOD ATR (<15min to close): same threshold but only fires in the last 15 minutes — captures rolled-over positions before overnight decay.
  • RULE 3 — EOD EMA10/20 CROSS: trend-failure exit. Calls close if EMA10 < EMA20; puts opposite.

Smart Exits requires the trade-token armed (TRADE: armed in header).

▶ ORDER TICKET TAB

Purpose: Place option orders (single legs) or stock orders. Submit / Preview / Clear are pinned to the bottom on phones, sticky on the right on laptop.

  • SYMBOL · TYPE — start here. Type=CALL is default; STOCK / ETF for shares.
  • LIVE QUOTE — bid / mid / ask / last / mark. Auto-refreshes when symbol changes.
  • STRIKE · EXPIRATION DATE — direct input. Or use AUTO-SELECT below.
  • ▲ AUTO-SELECT CONTRACT — pick a target month (or "Any 10–35 DTE"), click FIND BEST. Scores every contract by delta × OI × spread and fills the highest in the form.
  • ACTION · QTY · UP TO $ — BUY/SELL TO OPEN/CLOSE. Set "UP TO $" instead of QTY to size by budget; QTY auto-calculates as floor($ / (price × 100)).
  • ORDER TYPE — Market / Limit (default) / Stop / Stop Limit / Trailing Stop.
  • LIMIT PRICE — quick-set buttons: SET BID / SET MID / SET ASK based on the live quote.
  • DURATION — DAY / GTC / Pre / Post.
  • STEP FILL MODE (ON by default) — for buys: starts at BID, walks up toward ASK over N attempts to capture a better fill than just lifting the ask. Configurable: max attempts, fill wait, spread coverage %, fallback (give up / market).
  • PREVIEW ORDER — shows the broker-side preview without submitting. Always do this on first run.
  • ▲ SUBMIT ORDER — fires the order through Tradier. Live mode requires the trade token armed.
▶ API TAB

Purpose: Configure API keys. Default-collapsed because you set it once.

  • FMP API KEY — financialmodelingprep.com. Free tier: 250 calls/day. Required for Analyst Revisions ↑ + fundamentals.
  • TRADIER API KEY — for quotes + chains + history + order routing. Sandbox key for paper trading.
  • TRADIER MODE — SANDBOX (paper) or LIVE (real money). Mode propagates to all Tradier endpoints.
  • DEFAULT ACCOUNT NUMBER — auto-discovered on first PULL TRADIER from /v1/user/profile.
  • TRADIER PROXY URL (optional) — Cloudflare Worker that proxies Tradier so your key never lives in the browser. See worker/tradier-proxy/README.md.
  • TRADIER PROXY LIVE-MODE TOKEN — shared secret for the worker's LIVE_MODE_TOKEN gate. Defense-in-depth so a leaked workers.dev URL can't drain your live account.
  • SAVE KEYS / CLEAR KEYS — keys persist to localStorage on this device only.
  • CROSS-DEVICE SETTINGS SYNC (collapsed below) — encrypts your settings with a master secret you choose, stores in Cloudflare KV, restores on any device with the same secret. Trade token is excluded from sync (security-by-design).
  • API COUNTER — Tradier calls today, resets at 11:00 PM CST. Click for per-endpoint breakdown.
▶ PORTFOLIO TAB

Purpose: Multi-account portfolio aggregator. Drop in stock/option positions from multiple brokerages (Tradier, Fidelity, Schwab, IBKR, etc.) and see them in one view.

  • Click ↻ PULL TRADIER on the first column to auto-import live Tradier positions.
  • Click + ADD COLUMN to add another account (paste positions in any format — patterns: "100 AAPL @ 185", "1 NVDA 250 CALL 06/05/26", etc.).
  • EXPORT CSV — long-form, one row per position.
  • EXPORT MATRIX — wide-form: ticker rows, account columns; useful for spreadsheet aggregation.
  • RESET — clears all columns. Manual entries survive page reloads via localStorage.
▶ INDUSTRY TAB

Purpose: Theme rotation map. Shows daily / weekly / monthly composite scores for each industry theme (e.g. AI Infrastructure, Energy, Banks). Composite is built from price action, breadth, and breakout density across theme members.

  • INTRADAY / DAILY / WEEKLY / MONTHLY tabs — switch the time scale.
  • Themes are sorted by current score, top to bottom.
  • ↻ REFRESH — re-loads theme_scores.json from disk if you've regenerated it server-side.

The theme leaderboard ribbon at the top of every page (under the indices) is computed from this data; click any chip there to filter the scanner to that theme.

▶ GEX / FLOW TAB

Purpose: Gamma exposure (dealer positioning) and 30DTE call vs put OI flow for the top-200 ranked scan tickers. Drives the GEX Call Heavy scoring rule.

NET GEX — what it actually means for entries

"NET GEX" is a proxy for net dealer gamma exposure: (call OI − put OI) × price / 1,000,000 at the chosen 30DTE expiration. The SIGN tells you which way dealer hedging flows are leaning, which is the biggest single intraday volatility driver in heavily-optioned names:

  • POSITIVE NET GEX (call-heavy, dealers net LONG gamma): dealers SELL into rallies and BUY dips to stay neutral. This suppresses volatility — the stock pins to / oscillates around max-OI strikes. Long calls work best when the underlying is pushing through strong resistance with momentum (the suppression breaks). Short-premium / debit-spread setups can work too.
  • NEGATIVE NET GEX (put-heavy, dealers net SHORT gamma): dealers BUY into rallies and SELL into weakness. This amplifies volatility — moves accelerate in whichever direction price is heading. Long calls work best when you're catching a directional move just starting to unfold (a gamma squeeze). This is also where downside surprises get violent — useful to know before sizing.
  • Practical takeaway for long calls: you generally want to be LONG when (a) NET GEX is positive AND price is above the gamma flip strike (suppressed-vol regime → trend continuation favored), OR (b) NET GEX is strongly negative AND you have a clear directional catalyst (gamma squeeze acceleration). Avoid long calls in negative-GEX, no-catalyst chop — moves whipsaw.

GAMMA FLIP STRIKE — the level that matters

The gamma flip is the strike (within ±10% of spot) where the highest combined call+put OI sits. It's the price level where dealer net gamma typically flips sign. The mechanics:

  • ABOVE the flip strike: dealers tend to be net LONG gamma → suppress volatility → mean-revert. This is a "calm-bull" zone.
  • BELOW the flip strike: dealers tend to be net SHORT gamma → amplify moves → momentum. Acceleration zone.
  • AT the flip strike: highest pin risk. OI density there pulls price toward it as expiry approaches.
  • Long-call rule of thumb:
    • Best long-call setups: price just crossed UP through the flip with positive NET GEX — you've moved into the suppressed regime with momentum still pushing.
    • Riskier: price BELOW the flip with negative NET GEX — directional moves are large but you're fighting downside acceleration risk on any pullback.
    • Avoid: price pinned AT the flip with high OI — calls bleed theta while price oscillates around max-OI strike.

UI controls

  • RUN GEX — fetches 30-day options chain for top-200 tickers (~4 min, paced by Tradier rate limit). Auto-fires 20s after every scan.
  • AUTO schedule — Off / 1x at open (8:35 CST) / 3x daily (8:35, 11:00, 14:00 CST). Recommended: 3x — captures morning, lunch, and close-positioning shifts. Also runs headless via GitHub Actions cron 3× daily so updates happen even when the dashboard is closed.
  • Table — only bullish (call-heavy, ≥50% call OI) tickers shown, sorted most-bullish first by Call OI %, with |NET GEX| as tiebreaker (higher |GEX| = more conviction in the directional bias). PUT HEAVY tickers stay in G.gexData (rule scorecard still uses them) but suppressed from this display.
  • RUN pill on scanner rows — for tickers outside the top-200 batch. Click to fetch single-ticker GEX (~3s) so the GEX Call Heavy rule can score them.
▶ CONTRACT SCORE — BUY POPUP ON SCANNER PAGE

Purpose: When you click BUY on a scanner row, a popup lists every ATM-ish call across multiple expiries with a 0–100 SCORE. The score is a composite of three liquidity/quality signals — all unified through the internal _optScore(opt) helper:

  1. Delta score (40%): measures how directional the contract is. Targets the 0.65–0.85 delta band for the FIND BEST flow (deep-ITM enough to track underlying ~80%, with limited extrinsic decay). Outside that band the score drops sharply — a 0.20-delta lottery ticket and a 0.99-delta deep-ITM both score low for different reasons (too cheap/too leveraged vs. too expensive/too little leverage).
  2. Open interest score (35%): raw OI on a log scale. Higher OI = tighter spread, faster fills, better mid prices. 1,000+ OI gets full marks; under 100 docks the score significantly because spreads will eat your edge on entry and exit.
  3. Spread score (25%): (ask − bid) / mid. Tighter is better; anything wider than 5% of mid drops the score steeply. A 0.10-wide $5 contract scores great; a 0.30-wide $5 contract scores poorly because you'll give up real money on every round-trip.

The composite is rendered as a 0–100 number with color: green ≥65 (clean liquid contract), amber 45–64 (acceptable, watch the spread), red <45 (illiquid or off-target delta — hard to fill at fair value).

The popup also tags the ★ symbol next to the highest-scoring contract on each side so you can see which one the scanner would pick if you ran FIND BEST.

How to use the score

  • Sort by SCORE descending when comparing across expiries. The top row is your "least-friction" contract on a delta + liquidity basis.
  • Don't pick the absolute highest score blindly — also check the EXPIRY (a 7-day contract has high theta even with great score) and PREMIUM (within your budget).
  • Monthly expirations (3rd Friday) are highlighted with a ◆ glow because they're typically the most liquid; weekly contracts at the same delta/OI tier often score lower due to wider spreads.
▶ FIND BEST — ORDER TICKET AUTO-SELECT

What it does: The FIND BEST button (in the Order Ticket's AUTO-SELECT CONTRACT box) walks every available call expiry within your chosen window, pulls the chain for each, scores every contract via _optScore (same scoring as the BUY popup), and fills your form with the single highest-scoring contract.

How it picks

The selector targets a 0.70–0.85 delta range, a 10–35 calendar-day-to-expiry window (or whatever you set in the month dropdown), and the highest blended liquidity (OI × tightness of spread). Specifically:

  • Delta band: 0.70–0.85 is the "directional but not deep-ITM-decayed" sweet spot for swing-style long calls. At 0.75 delta the contract moves ~75¢ for every $1 in the underlying, so you get most of the upside with about 3–5× the leverage of shares, while ~10–25% of the premium is extrinsic (acceptable theta drag for 2–4 week holds).
  • DTE window: 10–35 days. Avoids the brutal final-week theta bleed under 7 DTE while staying short enough that movement-per-week is tradeable. The default "Any (10–35 DTE)" lets the picker try multiple expiries; pinning a specific month forces a single sweep.
  • Liquidity tiebreaker: when multiple contracts score similarly on delta, OI × (1 / spread%) wins. Tight spread + high OI matters more than a marginally better delta because fill quality determines real PnL.

When it's beneficial for long calls (vs not)

  • Use it when: you've already decided you want to go long the underlying. FIND BEST removes the "which strike do I pick?" friction and surfaces a contract that's deep enough to track the move and liquid enough to enter/exit cleanly.
  • Override when: you want a SPECIFIC strike (e.g. you're aiming at a known resistance level), a SPECIFIC expiry (catalyst-anchored — earnings, FOMC, scheduled event), or you want to size differently (e.g. 0.30-delta lottery for asymmetric upside, or 0.95-delta synthetic-stock equivalent).
  • Don't over-trust it for small-cap names: the "best" contract by score on an illiquid ticker can still have a $0.20 spread on a $1.50 mid. The score is RELATIVE within that ticker's chain. Read the BID/ASK after auto-fill.

FIND BEST result panel fields

  • Selected Strike / Expiry / Market Days — the picked contract.
  • GEX Signal — whether the underlying's call-vs-put OI flow is bullish (call-heavy) or bearish (put-heavy) — independent confirmation that institutional positioning is on your side.
  • Volume / Open Interest / Delta / Score — the same 0–100 composite explained in the BUY popup section above.
  • Candidates list — the next 2–3 runner-up contracts so you can see what was beat. Click any to swap.
▶ STRATEGY PRESETS — RATIONALE BEHIND EACH SETTING

Each preset applies a different combination of filter rules to surface a specific kind of setup. Thresholds re-calibrated 2026-05-03 against the observed score distribution: practical max is ~164 (cap), median GO scores 100–135, top decile sits ≈145+. Prior values (160 for everything, 180 for Best ITM) were above the 90th percentile of GO candidates which is why most presets returned 0–2 hits.

Empirical-evidence review of the rule weights

Highest-IC (information coefficient) rules in the scoring system, ranked by published swing-trade research:

  1. JT 12-1 Momentum (+20) — Jegadeesh-Titman 1993, 12-month return minus 1-month, top quintile. Strongest replicated edge in the system.
  2. Minervini Template (+15) — Mark Minervini's 8-criteria growth setup. High specificity for swing breakouts.
  3. Pocket Pivot (+15) — institutional accumulation signature; high signal-to-noise.
  4. VCP / Donchian / TF Aligned (+12 each) — volatility contraction, channel breakout, multi-timeframe trend.
  5. EMA20 > EMA50 / RS > SPY 21d / MFI / BBW (+10 each) — established trend + relative strength + flow.

2026-05-03 update: the 5 highest-IC rules (JT 12-1, Minervini, Pocket Pivot, VCP, Donchian) are now exposed as MUST-PASS chips. Each preset below has been upgraded to require a curated subset of these chips in addition to the previous criteria — the score threshold is no longer a proxy for "did the high-weight rules fire", it's an independent confirmation on TOP of the actual rule passes. The GO/NO-GO flag respects the active strategy's own Min Score (e.g. switching to HC reclassifies all GOs at threshold 140; switching to MOMENTUM reclassifies at 120).

★ HIGH CONVICTION

Filters: Signal=GO + Min Score ≥140 + Analyst Revisions ↑ + PROVEN ticker + GEX Call Heavy + JT 12-1 Mom + Minervini Trend Template.

Idea: the rarest, highest-quality setups. Every piece confirms a different dimension:

  • JT 12-1 Mom (NEW) — Jegadeesh-Titman 12-month-minus-1-month return ≥25% (top quintile). Strongest replicated cross-sectional edge in equity markets.
  • Minervini Trend Template (NEW) — full Stage 2 SEPA: price > MA50 > MA150 > MA200, MA200 trending up, RS rank ≥70, near 52wk hi. Mark Minervini's complete buy template, not just "above 200".
  • Score ≥140 — top of the actual score distribution. Was 145 in the prior version; lowered to 140 to compensate for the new rule MUST-PASS gates already eliminating low-quality candidates upstream.
  • Analyst Revisions ↑ — fundamental tailwind. Sell-side estimates being raised is a slow-moving but high-conviction signal that the company's outlook just improved.
  • PROVEN ticker — curated whitelist. Names that have historically responded predictably to this scoring system.
  • GEX Call Heavy — institutional positioning is on the long side.

Expect 0–5 GO candidates per scan. If nothing passes, the regime isn't right for high-conviction setups.

▲ BREAKOUT

Filters: Signal=GO + Min Score ≥125 + 52Wk Hi within 15% + RS > SPY + MACD + + TF Aligned 2/3 + Donchian 20d + VCP.

Idea: stocks pushing fresh highs with momentum + market leadership AND a confirmed pattern-based breakout. The Minervini / O'Neil school of breakout buying:

  • Donchian 20d (NEW) — close at or above 20-day high with confirming volume. Classic Turtle/channel breakout. Filters out names that LOOK extended but haven't actually printed a fresh 20-day high.
  • VCP (NEW) — Volatility Contraction Pattern (Minervini). Successive ATR-based price ranges contracting before the breakout. Without this, you'd buy "wide-and-loose" continuation moves that have lower hit rate.
  • 52Wk Hi <15% — proximity to 52-week high. George–Hwang (JF 2004) effect — return premium concentrated near the high.
  • RS > SPY — relative strength positive. Stock outperforming the index = market voting for it.
  • MACD + + TF Aligned 2/3 — short-term momentum + multi-timeframe agreement.

▶ MOMENTUM

Filters: Signal=GO + Min Score ≥120 + MACD + + Above EMA200 + RS > SPY + Strong Sector + JT 12-1 Mom.

Idea: strong trending stocks with sector tailwind, gated by the academic gold-standard cross-sectional momentum factor. Different from BREAKOUT in that you DON'T need 52-week-hi proximity — momentum can come from a mid-base extension or recovery off a base.

  • JT 12-1 Mom (NEW) — 12-month-minus-1-month return top quintile. Without this MUST-PASS, "MOMENTUM" was just "MACD + above 200" which is too lenient. Now it's "MACD + above 200 AND in the top fifth by 12-month return", which is the actual definition.
  • Above EMA200 — primary trend filter. Long bias only above the 200-day EMA.
  • Strong Sector — top-quartile theme score. Asness-Moskowitz cross-sectional momentum at the sector level.
  • MACD + + RS > SPY — short-term momentum + leadership confirmation.

◆ BEST ITM CALLS

Filters: Signal=GO + Min Score ≥145 + MACD + + Above EMA200 + RS > SPY + JT 12-1 Mom + Minervini + Pocket Pivot + Min Price $5 + Min AvgVol 1M + Today Vol >100% + Max Opt Price $8.

Idea: deep-ITM (delta 0.70–0.90) call buying on names that pass all three highest-IC scoring rules — JT 12-1 (+20), Minervini (+15), Pocket Pivot (+15). You're paying real premium for deep-ITM contracts, so the candidate has to clear the highest published bar across momentum, trend, and accumulation.

  • JT 12-1 + Minervini + Pocket Pivot (NEW) — the three rules with the strongest empirical track records, each capturing a different dimension (cross-sectional momentum, Stage 2 trend, institutional accumulation print). All three required.
  • Score ≥145 — top of the GO distribution. Was 150; lowered slightly because the three new MUST-PASS gates already concentrate quality upstream.
  • Min Price $5 + Min AvgVol 1M — liquidity floor. Cheap/illiquid names have wide option spreads that destroy edge.
  • Today Vol >100% — the move is happening NOW.
  • Max Opt Price $8 — keeps single-contract cost ≤$800.

▲ PRE/POST MARKET

Filters: SAME as HIGH CONVICTION (PROVEN + Score ≥140 + Analyst ↑ + GEX + JT 12-1 + Minervini) + secondary sort by absolute % gap (largest movers first).

Idea: extended-hours gappers screened with the conviction gate. Tradier's change_percentage reflects extended-hours quotes during 3–8:30am CST (pre) and 3–7pm CST (post); on weekends it shows the most recent extended-hours print. Use this preset to surface big movers that also pass HC criteria, not just any random gapper. The sort flip pushes biggest |gap %| to the top within the HC-passing subset.

Recommendations for new strategy presets (not yet built — note for future)

  • ★ FACTOR PURE — Score ≥130 + JT 12-1 + Minervini + Strong Sector + RS > SPY. Pure academic-factor stack with no chart-pattern dependence. Best for names where the visible setup is messy but the underlying factor profile is excellent.
  • ▲ COILED SPRING — Score ≥125 + VCP + Pocket Pivot + 52Wk Hi <15% + Vol > Avg. Tight pre-breakout coil with institutional accumulation print, then volume spike. Highest-conviction pre-breakout entry; alternative to BREAKOUT for "buying the spring" instead of "buying the breakout itself".
  • ▶ TURTLE — Score ≥125 + Donchian 20d + RS > SPY + Vol > Avg + > EMA200. Classic Dennis-Eckhardt 20d channel breakout, gated by leadership and primary-trend filters. Mechanical entry rule with strong out-of-sample edge.
  • ◆ BUY-THE-DIP — Score ≥130 + JT 12-1 + Minervini + RS > SPY (no breakout / 52Wk Hi requirement). Buys high-momentum quality names not at the highs — pulls from mid-base or post-correction setups. Pairs well with limit orders below current price.
  • ▲ ALL FIVE — Score ≥135 + JT 12-1 + Minervini + Pocket Pivot + VCP + Donchian. The "every Tier-1 chip MUST PASS" preset. Will return 0-1 names most days; useful as a daily "is anything truly perfect" probe rather than a working preset.
▶ WATCHLIST TAB

Purpose: Tickers you want to track outside the daily scan. Click any star ☆ on a scanner row to add the underlying.

  • Columns: SYMBOL · COMPANY · CHG % · SIGNAL · BUY · ✕ (remove).
  • ADD ticker — type a symbol, ENTER. COPY TICKERS — comma-separated to clipboard. CLEAR ALL — remove every ticker.
  • Click a row to open the candlestick chart on the right (laptop) or below (phone). EMA10/20/50 overlays + S/R bands.
  • DAILY / 1 MIN — chart timeframe.
▶ ALERTS TAB

Purpose: High-conviction signal notifications + auto-trader configuration + system event log.

  • ★ HIGH-CONVICTION ALERT SETTINGS (collapsed by default) — browser notifications, email via EmailJS (free 200/mo), min score threshold, cooldown, auto-fill order ticket, auto-submit countdown. Notifications fire after the bulk pass + stragglers cleanup completes (so no false signals on partial data).
  • AUTO-TRADER (collapsed by default) — fully automated execution. Master toggle, position size, daily loss limit, min score threshold, market-hours gate, max-open cap, market-order fallback. Designed for unattended scanning.
  • ▲ STRATEGY MODE — same presets as the FULL SCANNER tab, accessible here for one-click filter switching.
  • SYSTEM ALERTS LOG — running log of every notable event (scan complete, signal fired, order submitted, fills, errors). Click CLEAR to reset.
▶ GLOSSARY — ABBREVIATIONS & INDICATORS
  • ATR(14) — Average True Range over 14 days. Measures volatility; used for stop placement.
  • DTE — Days To Expiration.
  • EMA10 / EMA20 / EMA50 / EMA200 — Exponential Moving Averages of close price. EMA10 > EMA20 > EMA50 > EMA200 = strong stacked uptrend.
  • GEX (Gamma Exposure) — net dealer gamma positioning derived from call OI − put OI normalized by price.
  • MACD + — MACD histogram positive (12-EMA above 26-EMA).
  • OI (Open Interest) — total open contracts at a strike. Higher OI = more liquid.
  • PROVEN ticker — on the curated whitelist of names that have historically responded well to this scoring system. Built into the universe.
  • RS 5D — 5-day relative strength vs SPY (your stock's % change minus SPY's % change). Positive = outperforming.
  • RTH / PRE / POST / CLOSED — Regular Trading Hours (8:30am–3:00pm CST) / Pre-market (3:00–8:30am CST) / Post-market (3:00–7:00pm CST) / outside both.
  • TF Aligned 2/3 — Time-Frame Aligned. Counts how many of three trend timeframes (daily / hourly / 15-min) point the same direction. ≥2/3 passes.
  • U/D Volume — Up-volume / down-volume ratio. >1.5 = institutional accumulation.
  • VCP — Volatility Contraction Pattern (Minervini). Tightening price ranges before breakout.
▶ DATA SOURCES & CACHING

Every external data source has a localStorage cache to minimize API calls. TTLs:

  • Scan results — 30 minutes. Restored on tab switch and page reload.
  • GEX/Flow — 24 hours. Persists overnight; OI moves daily.
  • Positions — 5 minutes. Click REFRESH on the Positions tab for live.
  • Tradier expirations — 6 hours (intraday-stable).
  • Tradier history — 24 hours (daily bars don't change intraday).
  • FMP fundamentals — 24 hours per ticker.
  • Earnings calendar — 12 hours.
  • Theme overlay (theme_scores.json) — 6 hours.
  • Header indices quotes — 60s refresh during 3:00am–7:00pm CST (extended hours window).

Tradier rate limit: 95/min (kept under the proxy's 100/min/IP gate). The fetch wrapper queues calls and respects 429 Retry-After headers automatically.

▶ TROUBLESHOOTING
  • "%'s not showing for indices" — header indices auto-refresh every 60s during 3am–7pm CST. Outside that window, last cached values display. If still empty, hit VERIFY in the STRATEGY row to confirm Tradier is responding.
  • "Scanner shows random tickers" — fixed 2026-05-02. If you see them, hard-reload (Cmd+Shift+R / Ctrl+Shift+R) to bust browser cache.
  • "⚠ data gaps after scan" — informational. Top 60 tickers always fully enrich; ranks 61–200 fill in via background pass over the next ~2 minutes. Tradier history takes longer than FMP because indicator-history fetches are paced harder to avoid 429s.
  • "Buy notification fired before scan finished" — fixed 2026-05-03. Notifications now fire only after the stragglers cleanup pass completes (~3–5s after scan-complete), not on partial data.
  • "GEX not caching" — TTL bumped to 24h on 2026-05-03. If you reload within 24h of a successful runGex, the data hydrates immediately. Older cache may need one fresh runGex to populate the new format.
  • "Indices wrap to multiple rows on small windows" — they horizontal-scroll on laptop now. iPhone shows them in a single horizontal-scroll strip below the wordmark.
  • "Sectors / Themes dropdown hidden behind table" — fixed 2026-05-03 via position:fixed. Hard-reload if still occurring.
  • "STEX score 79 ranks above TEAM score 100" — you have the PRE/POST MARKET preset active, which sorts by absolute gap % instead of score. Click ✕ CLEAR in the STRATEGY row to restore score-based ranking.
  • Order Ticket SUBMIT not visible on iPhone — bottom action bar pinned to viewport bottom; should always be tappable. Form scrolls above it.
  • Positions table doesn't show ATR-anchored exit prices — opens before this feature shipped have no underlyingAtOpen snapshot. Manually set via console: G.posOpenData['SYM_OCC'].underlyingAtOpen = 195.50; will recompute on next render.
Build issues? See github.com/alexreed122287/scanner · This tab is a static reference, no API calls.

API CONFIGURATION
Keys stored only in your browser localStorage — never leave your device.
FMP: financialmodelingprep.com  |  Tradier: developer.tradier.com
CROSS-DEVICE SETTINGS SYNC
Encrypts your scanner settings with a master secret you choose, stores in Cloudflare KV. Use the same master secret on any other device to PULL down identical settings.
Master secret never leaves this device. Worker only stores ciphertext. Trade token is excluded from sync — re-arm per device for security.
API USAGE ANALYZER
Click ANALYZE to inspect your call log and get optimization recommendations.
▲ GEX / FLOW 30DTE options-flow (call vs put OI) for top 200 ranked tickers
▾ AUTO
Click RUN to fetch flow data
▾ BULLISH ENTRY REFERENCE — what to look for
RANKING ORDER (top of list = best long-call entry)
  1. FLOW = CALL HEAVY only (put-heavy + balanced filtered out)
  2. NET GEX highest to lowest
  3. CALL OI % highest to lowest (90%+ floats to top)
  4. CALL OI highest to lowest (liquidity filter)
  5. STOCK PRICE above GAMMA FLIP only (dealer-long zone)
  6. DTE 30-45d sweet spot
  7. STRIKE within delta 0.70-0.80 range that fits your budget
COLUMN QUICK REFERENCE
SYMBOLunderlying ticker
STOCK PRICEcurrent spot — must be ABOVE gamma flip for bullish setups
NET GEX ↑higher = better (positive = dealers long gamma, suppress vol)
CALL OI ↑want >1K (liquidity)
PUT OIignore for long-call setups
GAMMA FLIPstock price needs to be above this
CALL OI % ↑want 90%+ (extreme call dominance)
PUT OI %lower better
DTEwant 30-45d sweet spot
EXPexpiration date of the chain
STRIKEgamma flip strike — pick a contract above this
FLOWcall heavy only
Rows highlighted in CYAN GLOW meet ALL bullish criteria simultaneously: NET GEX positive, CALL OI % ≥ 90, CALL OI > 1K, stock price above gamma flip, DTE 30-45.
▲

Click RUN GEX to fetch 30DTE options-flow data for the top 200 ranked scan results.
~400 Tradier calls, ~4 min, paced by rate limit.
Auto-fires 20s after every scan.

✓ METHOD 1 — GUIDED BUILDER (RECOMMENDED)
Fill fields → ADD → row lands in the staging list below. Repeat for each position, then IMPORT.
📄 METHOD 2 — CSV TEMPLATE
Cols: KIND, TICKER, STRIKE, EXPIRATION, TYPE, QTY, AVG_PRICE (KIND=option/shares; option rows need strike/exp/type; shares rows need avg_price)
METHOD 3 — PASTE / FREE-FORM (FALLBACK)

Handles Robinhood's native 2-line paste format. Separate positions with a blank line. Missing years are auto-inferred to the next upcoming M/D.

Examples accepted:
CXAI $0.5 Call
5/15 · 133 buys

VLN $1 Call
11/20 · 1 buy

AAPL
50 shares · Avg $175.42

NVDA $150 Call 12/19/2025 x3 (single line)
AMZN,220,2026-01-16,CALL,2 (CSV-style)

Note: Robinhood shows "N buys" — this is purchase count, NOT contract qty. Parsed positions default to qty=1; adjust after import.

Staged positions will appear here.

🔒 TRADE AUTHORIZATION
Enter session trade token
Required to place or cancel orders this session.
Default: held in memory only — re-prompted next session.
Must match the worker's WRITE_AUTH_TOKEN secret.
Stores in this device's browser storage, restores across reloads + sleeps + reboots. Auto-expires after 14 days of inactivity. Anyone with access to this device + browser can place orders. Only check on a personal device with a passcode.
Cancel = scan-only mode (no order placement until re-armed)
